Common.noRarityCaption: No public rarity label shown on the official card.
Common.previewUnavailable: Preview image not yet released — Trello currently shows a TBA placeholder for this skin.

Skins.metaTitle: Official Bizarre Lineage Skins | {count} Trello Listings
Skins.metaDescription: Browse the {count} skin entries currently listed on the official Bizarre Lineage Trello, with direct card links and official preview art where available.
Skins.heroIntro: The public official Trello currently lists {skinCount} skin entries across {standCount} Stand families. This page mirrors those listings directly, including official preview art and per-card source links.
Skins.howToGetBody: The official Lucky Arrow item card says that a Lucky Arrow guarantees a random skin for your currently equipped Stand. The same official item card currently lists three acquisition paths: Prestige Shop, Raid Shops, and Legendary Chests.

faq.q1: Do skins affect my Stand's stats?
faq.a1: No. Skins in Bizarre Lineage are purely cosmetic. They change the visual appearance of your Stand but do not modify any stats, moves, or abilities.
faq.q2: Why do some entries have no rarity badge?
faq.a2: The public official Trello currently shows an explicit Common label on some skin cards, but many cards are unlabeled. This page avoids guessing the missing rarity values and links every entry back to its official source card.
faq.q3: Where do these previews come from?
faq.a3: The preview art on this page is pulled from the public official Trello card attachments whenever the card exposes one. If a card has no attached preview image, the entry stays listed here but shows an image placeholder instead.

Perks.metaTitle: Bizarre Lineage Perks & Traits — All Sources (Official Trello)
Perks.metaDescription: All perks and traits in Bizarre Lineage as documented on the official Trello, including Money Store, Tutorial, Prestige, side quest, gang, raid, and Stand-specific traits.
Perks.heroIntro: All {count} perks and traits documented on the official Trello, grouped by source: shops, tutorial, prestige, side quests, gangs, raids, and Stand-specific trait sections.
Perks.aboutBody: Effect text comes from the public official Trello Traits card. Some token costs also appear in raid shop data, but this page does not invent missing drop rates, roll odds, or best-in-slot rankings.
Perks.effectNotDocumented: Effect not documented on Trello — verify in-game.
Perks.sourceBody: Verified against the public official Trello Traits card. If a perk or trait you have seen in-game is missing here, it likely has not been added to the public Trello yet.
Perks.relatedRaidsSub: Earn tokens to buy raid-shop traits
Perks.relatedBuildPlannerSub: Plan your Stand + Style + Sub combo

SubAbilities.metaTitle: Bizarre Lineage Sub-Abilities | Official Moves + Planner Notes
SubAbilities.metaDescription: Bizarre Lineage Hamon, Vampire, and Cyborg with official Trello source links for obtainment and moves; PvP/PvE scores and synergies are site-maintained planner notes.
SubAbilities.heroIntro: Sub-abilities add passives and active moves documented on public Trello cards. Scores and Stand pairings on this site are planner estimates, not official balance values.
SubAbilities.detailMetaDescription: Official Trello obtainment and move notes for {name}, with site-maintained planner scores and build synergies clearly labeled.
SubAbilities.detailDisclaimer: Passive and move notes are cross-checked against the public official Trello where available. Scores and synergies below are site-maintained planner notes, not official balance values.
SubAbilities.sourceBoundary: Official Trello cards support obtainment notes, passive text, and move names where available. PvP/PvE/survival scores and Top Synergies are site-maintained planner data.

FightingStyles.metaTitle: Bizarre Lineage Fighting Styles | Official Moves + Planner Notes
FightingStyles.metaDescription: Bizarre Lineage Boxing, Kendo, and Karate with official Trello source links for obtainment and moves; scores and Stand synergies are site-maintained planner notes.
FightingStyles.heroIntro: Fighting styles are documented on the public official Trello through Boxing, Kendo, and Karate cards. Scores and best-with pairings on this site are planner estimates, not official balance values.
FightingStyles.detailMetaTitle: {name} Fighting Style Guide | Bizarre Lineage Wiki
FightingStyles.detailMetaDescription: Official Trello obtainment and moves for {name}, with site-maintained planner scores and Stand synergies clearly labeled.
FightingStyles.detailDisclaimer: Official Trello is used for obtainment notes and move names where available. Scores and synergies below are site-maintained planner notes, not official balance values.
FightingStyles.sourceBoundary: Official Trello cards support obtainment notes and move names. Combo/damage scores and Best With pairings are site-maintained planner data.

Vault.metaTitle: Build Vault — Save and Compare Bizarre Lineage Builds
Vault.metaDescription: Your saved Bizarre Lineage builds. Store Stand + fighting style + sub-ability combos from the Build Planner and compare their scores side by side.
Vault.heroIntro: Your saved builds from the Build Planner. Stored locally in your browser — compare builds side by side, remove what no longer fits, or export a shareable link.
Vault.subtitle: {count} stored builds. Data is saved locally in this browser.
Vault.importedNoneNew: No new builds found in the imported file.
Vault.importedFailParse: Failed to parse the backup file.
Vault.emptyBody: Create and save planner builds to store them here.

Compare.metaTitle: Compare Bizarre Lineage Stands Side by Side
Compare.metaDescription: Compare two or three Bizarre Lineage Stands side by side — stats, moves, tier, recommended builds, and counters.
Compare.heroIntro: Pick two or three Stands to compare their stats, recommended builds, and counters side by side. Community planner data, cross-checked against the official Trello for move names.
Compare.selectBuildsBody: Go to your Vault and select exactly two builds to compare them side-by-side.
Compare.verdictDisclaimer: These differences come from the site's local planner scores, not official balance data.

Trello.metaTitle: Official Bizarre Lineage Trello — Direct Links to All Cards
Trello.metaDescription: Jump straight to the official Bizarre Lineage Trello board. Direct links to Stand, item, perk, and event cards verified from the public board.
Trello.heroIntro: The official Trello board is the primary source of truth for Bizarre Lineage game data. It is maintained by the developer MIDAS and contains verified information about Stands, raids, progression, and upcoming content.
Trello.ctaSub: Bookmark this link for quick access to verified game data.
Trello.discordSub: Community chat, code drops, and announcements
Trello.section1Desc: Official move names, obtainment methods, and evolution paths for all Stands. The Trello is the only source that confirms exact move names.
Trello.section1ConfirmedItems: Move names and keys|Obtainment methods (Arrow, Evolution, Quest)|Evolution chains (e.g. Whitesnake → C-Moon → Made in Heaven)
Trello.section1NotItems: Exact drop rates / percentages|Tier rankings|DPS numbers
Trello.section2Desc: Raid boss names, locations, NPC interactions, and shop items are all documented on the Trello.
Trello.section2ConfirmedItems: Boss names and locations|Raid NPC names and Bus Stop numbers|Shop items and token costs|Grading system (SSS to D)
Trello.section2NotItems: Exact boss HP values|Optimal strategies|Drop rate percentages
Trello.section3Desc: Core mechanics like leveling, prestige, stats, conjuration, and fighting styles.
Trello.section3ConfirmedItems: Prestige requirements (Level 50, Arch Mage, 10,000 Cash)|Prestige location (Bus Stop 10)|Stat types and general effects|Fighting style and sub-ability names
Trello.section3NotItems: Exact stat scaling formulas|Hidden mechanics|Balance change history
Trello.section4Desc: Upcoming features, planned content, and known bugs that the dev team is tracking.
Trello.section4ConfirmedItems: Planned new Stands|Upcoming features|Known bugs and fixes
Trello.section4NotItems: Release dates (subject to change)|Exact implementation details
Trello.faqQ1: Is the Trello always up to date?
Trello.faqA1: The Trello is maintained by the developer and is generally updated around major patches. However, minor changes or hotfixes may not be reflected immediately.
Trello.faqQ2: How is this wiki different from the Trello?
Trello.faqA2: The Trello provides raw official data. This wiki adds community-maintained tier rankings, build recommendations, strategy guides, and interactive tools like the Build Planner that the Trello does not offer.

BuildPlanner.metaTitle: Bizarre Lineage Build Planner | Site-Maintained Scores
BuildPlanner.metaDescription: Bizarre Lineage build planner using site-maintained scoring rules. Official Trello is used for move and obtainment references; scores are not official balance values.
BuildPlanner.heroIntro: Combine a Stand with a fighting style and sub-ability — the planner estimates performance across 5 dimensions. Save builds to your Vault and compare profiles side by side. Numbers come from the site's local dataset, not official balance.
BuildPlanner.emptyStateText: Select a Stand to view its planner estimate.
BuildPlanner.plannerDisclaimer: Planner estimates are derived from local site data and weighting rules, not official balance values.
BuildPlanner.incompleteAddStyle: Your build is incomplete — add a Fighting Style to unlock its full potential.
BuildPlanner.incompleteAddSub: Your build is incomplete — add a Sub-Ability to unlock its full potential.
BuildPlanner.tryDifferentStyle: Try a different style to push this build higher.
BuildPlanner.tryDifferentSub: Try a different sub-ability to push this build higher.
BuildPlanner.saveBuildHint: Don't lose this build — save it and compare later.
BuildPlanner.warningSurvival: Low survivability ({score}/10) — Vampire sub-ability would add lifesteal and regen.
BuildPlanner.warningMobility: Very low mobility ({score}/10) — {name} can't chase or escape. Commit to fights carefully.
BuildPlanner.warningBothLow: Below average in both PvP ({pvp}) and PvE ({pve}). Consider a different Stand.
BuildPlanner.warningNoStyle: No Fighting Style selected — you're missing +0.5-1.0 score from style bonuses.
BuildPlanner.warningNoSub: No Sub-Ability selected — you're missing Survival and PvP/PvE modifiers.
BuildPlanner.warningStyleMismatch: {currentStyle} isn't the recommended style for {name}. Try {recommendedStyle} for better synergy.
BuildPlanner.oneStepStyleHint: The local planner often starts this setup with {style} as a first style to test.
BuildPlanner.oneStepSubHint: The local planner often pairs this build with {sub} as a first sub-ability to try.
BuildPlanner.fallbackIntro: Compare site-maintained build estimates across 5 dimensions: PvP, PvE, Survival, Mobility, and Accessibility. The interactive planner is loading below.
BuildPlanner.fallbackBox1Body: Browse the current local dataset and start from any stand profile.
BuildPlanner.fallbackBox2Body: Test Boxing, Kendo, and Karate against different stand setups.
BuildPlanner.fallbackBox3Body: Layer Hamon, Vampire, or Cyborg into the same build profile.
BuildPlanner.fallbackWhat1: Scores a stand, style, and sub-ability combination using the site's local planner rules.
BuildPlanner.fallbackWhat2: Highlights suggested pairings so you can compare a default setup against your own build idea.
BuildPlanner.fallbackWhat3: Saves finished setups to your local browser vault after the interactive controls load.
BuildPlanner.howToUseTitle: How to Use the Bizarre Lineage Build Planner
BuildPlanner.howToUse1Body: Choose from {count} Stands in the database. Each Stand has unique base scores for Damage, Combo, CC, AoE, Mobility, and Sustain that feed into the planner calculations.
BuildPlanner.howToUse2Body: Pair your Stand with Boxing, Kendo, or Karate for fighting style, and Hamon, Vampire, or Cyborg for sub-ability. Each combination changes your PvP, PvE, and Survival scores differently.
BuildPlanner.howToUse3Body: See real-time site-maintained scores across 5 dimensions. Save builds to your Vault and compare up to 2 builds side-by-side; these scores are planning estimates, not official rankings.
BuildPlanner.scoreCardPvpBody: Site-maintained weighted combination of Damage (50%), CC (30%), and Combo (20%), plus style and sub modifiers. Higher means stronger under this planner formula, not an official PvP rating.
BuildPlanner.scoreCardPveBody: Site-maintained weighting by AoE (60%) and Damage (40%). It estimates mission, raid, and world-event convenience but is not an official farming score.
BuildPlanner.scoreCardSurvivalBody: Based on local Stand sustain plus sub-ability survival rating. This is planner weighting, not a developer-published survival stat.
BuildPlanner.scoreCardMobilityBody: Uses the local Stand mobility score. Useful for planning chase/escape, but not an official stat table.
BuildPlanner.scoreCardCostBody: Uses local rarity/accessibility assumptions. It does not represent official drop percentages.
BuildPlanner.scoreCardGradeBody: The badge shows S+/S/A/B/C/D from the highest local planner dimension in your build, not an official build grade.
BuildPlanner.faqQ1: Is the Build Planner based on official game data?
BuildPlanner.faqA1: Move names and obtainment methods come from the official Trello board. The scoring weights and tier rankings are site-maintained community estimates, not official balance values.
BuildPlanner.faqQ2: Are my saved builds stored on a server?
BuildPlanner.faqA2: No. All builds are saved in your browser's local storage. They are never uploaded. Use the Export feature to back up your vault as a JSON file.
BuildPlanner.faqA3: We cross-check the official Trello after each game update. The scoring formula and tier placements are reviewed when the meta shifts significantly.
BuildPlanner.faqQ4: Can I share a build with someone?
BuildPlanner.faqA4: Yes. Copy the URL from your browser after selecting a Stand, Style, and Sub — the selections are encoded in the URL query parameters. Anyone opening that link will see the same build loaded.

About.metaTitle: About Bizarre Lineage Wiki — Who We Are
About.metaDescription: About the Bizarre Lineage Wiki — a fan-maintained tools site that cross-checks every entry against the public official Trello board.
About.body: Bizarre Lineage Wiki is a fan-maintained tools site for the Roblox game Bizarre Lineage. We are not affiliated with the developers, MIDAS, or Roblox. Every official move name, obtainment path, and progression note on this site is cross-checked against the public official Trello board at trello.com/b/wtzgwqIf. Rankings, planner scores, and build notes are community-maintained and clearly labeled as site-local — they are not official balance values. If something on this site contradicts the public Trello, the Trello wins. For bug reports, typos, or data corrections, email redmanhao@gmail.com.

Contact.metaDescription: Contact the Bizarre Lineage Wiki team — bug reports, typos, data corrections, and feedback.
Contact.body: For bug reports, typos, data corrections, or general feedback, email redmanhao@gmail.com. If you found a fact on this site that contradicts the public official Trello, please include the page URL and the Trello card link — we will fix it fast.

Privacy.metaDescription: Privacy policy for Bizarre Lineage Wiki. Learn how we use cookies, handle data, and work with third-party advertisers.
Privacy.noteTranslated: Translation provided as a courtesy. The English version is the authoritative legal text — questions: redmanhao@gmail.com.
Privacy.intro: Your privacy is important to us. This Privacy Policy explains how Bizarre Lineage Wiki ("we", "us", or "our") collects, uses, and protects your information when you visit our website at bizarrelineage.com.
Privacy.section1Body: Our website uses cookies — small text files stored on your device — to enhance your browsing experience. We use the following types of cookies:
Privacy.cookieEssential: Essential Cookies: Required for basic site functionality, such as remembering your preferences.
Privacy.cookieAnalytics: Analytics Cookies: Help us understand how visitors interact with our site (e.g., Google Analytics). These cookies may collect anonymized data such as page views, session duration, and referring URLs.
Privacy.cookieAds: Advertising Cookies: Used by third-party advertising partners (see Section 3 below) to serve personalized ads and measure ad performance.
Privacy.cookieControl: You can manage or disable cookies through your browser settings at any time. Please note that disabling certain cookies may affect website functionality.
Privacy.section2Body: When you use the Build Planner and click "Save to Vault", all data regarding your specific Stand, Style, and Sub-Ability combination is saved entirely on your local device using standard browser localStorage. We do not transmit, collect, or store your builds on our servers. You retain complete control over this data, and clearing your browser cache will permanently delete your stored builds.
Privacy.section3Body1: We use Google AdSense and potentially other third-party advertising networks to display ads on our website. These advertising partners may use cookies and similar tracking technologies to collect information about your visits to this and other websites in order to provide advertisements about goods and services of interest to you.
Privacy.section3Body2: Google's use of advertising cookies enables it and its partners to serve ads based on your visit to our site and/or other sites on the Internet. You may opt out of personalized advertising by visiting Google Ads Settings.
Privacy.section4Body: We do not require you to create an account or provide personal information to use our site. However, through cookies and analytics tools, we may automatically collect:
Privacy.collect4: Pages visited and time spent on each page
Privacy.section4Closing: This data is used solely to improve our website and understand our audience. We do not sell your personal data to any third party.
Privacy.section5Body: Depending on your location, you may have certain rights regarding your data, including:
Privacy.right1: The right to know what data is collected about you
Privacy.right2: The right to request deletion of your data
Privacy.right3: The right to opt out of the sale or sharing of your personal information
Privacy.right4: The right to opt out of personalized advertising
Privacy.section5Closing: To exercise any of these rights, please contact us at redmanhao@gmail.com.
Privacy.section6Body: Our website is not directed at children under the age of 13. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If you believe that a child has provided us with personal data, please contact us and we will take steps to delete such information.
Privacy.section7Body: Our website may contain links to external sites (such as public Trello boards or YouTube guides) that are not operated by us. Please be aware that we have no control over the content and privacy practices of these sites, and this Privacy Policy does not apply to them.
Privacy.section8Body: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Any changes will be posted on this page with a revised "Last updated" date. We encourage you to review this page periodically for the latest information.
Privacy.section9Body: If you have any questions or concerns about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at:

Terms.metaTitle: Terms of Service | Bizarre Lineage Wiki
Terms.metaDescription: Terms of service for Bizarre Lineage Wiki — usage rights, disclaimers, and user responsibilities.
Terms.noteTranslated: Translation provided as a courtesy. The English version is the authoritative legal text — questions: redmanhao@gmail.com.
Terms.intro: By accessing Bizarre Lineage Wiki, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree with any part of these terms, please do not use our website.
Terms.section1Body: Bizarre Lineage Wiki provides informational content and calculators for the Roblox game "Bizarre Lineage". The service is provided "as is" and intended solely for personal, non-commercial use by players mapping out video game strategies.
Terms.section2Body: The materials appearing on Bizarre Lineage Wiki could include technical, typographical, or photographic errors. We do not warrant that any of the materials on its website are accurate, complete, or current. We may make changes to the materials contained on its website at any time without notice, particularly as the base game receives balance patches.
Terms.section3Body: In no event shall Bizarre Lineage Wiki be liable for any damages (including, without limitation, "lost" in-game items, currency, or time) arising out of the use or inability to use the materials on our website.

Guides.metaDescription: Browse all Bizarre Lineage guides: stats explained, official acquisition limits, site-maintained build notes, leveling routes, and prestige requirements.
Guides.heroIntro: These guides mix public official Trello notes with site-maintained planner guidance. Pick a guide below based on whether you need verified progression info or local build suggestions.
Guides.cardBeginnerDesc: Official Roblox and Trello facts for new players: controls, Stand Arrow, Lucky Arrow, Prestige, and World Events.
Guides.cardMadeInHeavenTitle: How to Get Made in Heaven
Guides.cardMadeInHeavenDesc: Officially confirmed Made in Heaven facts: Trello evolution listing, Special label, Pucci location, and public Journey to Heaven requirement.
Guides.cardStatsDesc: What each stat does, plus site-maintained starter presets for PvP, PvE, and beginner setups.
Guides.cardStandChancesDesc: See how this site groups Stands by rarity and what the public official Trello does and does not confirm about Stand acquisition.
Guides.cardBestBuildsDesc: Site-maintained planner suggestions for Stand + Style + Sub setups. These are not official developer recommendations.
Guides.cardAwakeningDesc: Source-aware awakening notes that separate public Trello requirements from site-maintained strategy suggestions.
Guides.cardNightVampireDesc: Official base Vampire facts plus the public-source boundary for unconfirmed Night Vampire upgrade claims.
Guides.cardLevelingDesc: Official Trello-based progression notes plus a safer checklist for early leveling priorities.
Guides.cardPrestigeDesc: Officially documented prestige requirements, rewards, and notes on what the public Trello currently confirms.

stats.metaTitle: Bizarre Lineage Stats Guide — Damage, HP, Speed
stats.metaDescription: What every stat does in Bizarre Lineage, plus site-maintained stat presets for PvP, PvE, and beginner setups.

standChances.metaTitle: Bizarre Lineage Stand Chances & Rarity Guide
standChances.metaDescription: How Stands are grouped by rarity and what the public Trello confirms or does not confirm about Stand acquisition in Bizarre Lineage.

bestBuilds.metaTitle: Bizarre Lineage Build Ideas — Site Planner Setups
bestBuilds.metaDescription: Site-maintained Bizarre Lineage build ideas for Stand + Fighting Style + Sub-Ability setups, clearly labeled as planner suggestions rather than official recommendations.

prestige.metaTitle: Bizarre Lineage Prestige Guide — Requirements & Rewards
prestige.metaDescription: Full Bizarre Lineage Prestige guide — level requirements, what resets, what carries over, and what the Prestige Shop sells.
prestige.intro: The public official Trello confirms that prestiging is an end-game mechanic handled by the Arch Mage. This page sticks to the details that are explicitly documented there.
prestige.requirementsIntro: The public official Trello currently confirms the following:
prestige.reqNpc: NPC: Talk to the Arch Mage in the Hospital.
prestige.reqLocation: Location: The official Trello places the Arch Mage near Bus Stop 10.
prestige.benefit1: 5 Prestige Shards: The official card says the Arch Mage grants five Prestige Shards.
prestige.benefit2: 1 Stand Storage: The same card says you also receive one Stand storage.
prestige.benefit3: Prestige Shop access: The Arch Mage also provides access to the Prestige Shop.
prestige.lose1: The public Trello does not publish a complete lose-on-prestige checklist.
prestige.lose2: Verify reset behavior in-game before prestiging instead of relying on third-party tables.
prestige.keep1: The public Trello does not publish a full keep-on-prestige checklist either.
prestige.keep2: What is clearly documented publicly is the 10,000 Cash cost, Arch Mage location, Prestige Shards, Stand storage, and Prestige Shop access.

leveling.metaTitle: Bizarre Lineage Leveling Guide — Official Progression Notes
leveling.metaDescription: Bizarre Lineage leveling guide built around public official Trello progression notes and clearly labeled unknowns.
leveling.intro: This guide focuses on the progression steps that are explicitly described on the public official Trello. It avoids undocumented NPC routes and level brackets that can drift out of date.
leveling.bracket1Body: The public Trello does not document a verified level-1-10 NPC route here. What it does confirm is that your first step should be the Tutorial at the Morioh Grand Hotel by talking to the Receptionist.
leveling.bracket1Source: The Tutorial entry on the public Trello explains where to start and which area to visit first.
leveling.bracket2Body: The public Trello does not confirm this exact bracket as an official route. What it does confirm is that early progression depends on unlocking your first Stand with a Stand Arrow and building around the move set you pull.
leveling.bracket2Source: Check the public Trello cards for Stand Arrow and the early progression section.
leveling.bracket3Body: The public Trello does not publish this exact mid-game route, but it does confirm that missions, kills, ranked wins, and PvP missions all build Stand conjuration. It also points to the gym mat as a strong conjuration farm.
leveling.bracket3Source: The progression cards describe mission boards and conjuration farming in the gym.
leveling.bracket4Body: The public Trello does not confirm a single official late-game farming route on this page. What it does document clearly is where Boxing, Kendo, Karate, Hamon, Vampire, and Cyborg come from once you are ready to expand your build.
leveling.bracket4Source: Use the official Trello obtainment cards for each trainer or item path instead of outdated community route lists.
leveling.proTip1: Mission Boards: The official Trello calls them one of the most important early-game progression tools.
leveling.proTip2: Lucky Arrow: The official Lucky Arrow card is cosmetic; it guarantees a random skin on your current Stand.
leveling.proTip3: Prestige: The public Trello confirms you prestige through the Arch Mage for 10,000 Cash near Bus Stop 10.

awakening.metaTitle: Bizarre Lineage Awakening Guide — How to Awaken Your Stand
awakening.metaDescription: How to awaken your Stand in Bizarre Lineage, the requirements per Stand, and what Awakening unlocks.
awakening.intro: Awakening notes are limited to what the public official Trello confirms: Inner World, gym mat entry, Level 50, Stand Conjuration 100, and the Inner World clone option.
awakening.step1Body: You must be Level 50 before you can begin the awakening quest. Use the leveling guide for official progression notes and clearly labeled unknowns.
awakening.step1Tip: Grind quests at Bus Stop areas that match your level range for maximum XP efficiency.
awakening.step2Body: Train Conjuration at the Gym Mat in Morioh until it reaches 100. This is required to interact with the Self NPC.
awakening.step2Tip: Conjuration training is AFK-friendly. Hold the interaction and let it run while you step away.
awakening.step3Body: Find the Self NPC in the Meditation Realm. This NPC appears only when you meet both level and conjuration requirements.
awakening.step3Tip: The Meditation Realm entrance is near the Gym Mat area in Morioh.
awakening.step4Title: Choose the Inner World awakening option
awakening.step4Body: The public Inner World card says the option "I want to surpass my limits" makes you fight yourself with the awakening of your Stand and unlocks awakening.
awakening.step4Tip: The public card does not say Jotaro or DIO are awakening requirements.
awakening.step5Body: After the official Trello facts end, any strategy, matchup, or best-Stand advice should be treated as site-maintained planner guidance rather than official data.
awakening.step5Tip: Do not add boss HP, fastest-route claims, or cooldown values without an official card or dated in-game test.
awakening.boss1Tip: The official card confirms you fight yourself for the awakening option; it does not publish HP.
awakening.boss2Tip: The public official cards checked here do not list Jotaro or DIO as awakening requirements.
awakening.boss3Tip: Planner strategy can be useful, but it must be labeled separately from official requirements.
awakening.bestStands1: Official facts — Inner World, gym mat entry, Level 50, Stand Conjuration 100, and the Inner World clone option are public Trello facts.
awakening.bestStands2: Site-maintained guidance — best Stand, best style, and matchup advice belong to planner data, not official developer data.
awakening.bestStands3: Unconfirmed — boss HP, exact clear time, and fastest route are not published on the public official cards checked here.
awakening.faqA1: The public Inner World card says the awakening option unlocks awakening, and Roblox describes awakening as part of the game. It does not publish a full official per-Stand awakening table on this page.
awakening.faqQ2: Do I lose my awakening when I prestige?
awakening.faqA2: The public official cards checked here do not state a prestige reset rule for awakening. Treat any reset claim as unconfirmed unless separately sourced.
awakening.faqA3: H is the Roblox-listed Awaken / Ultimate input. Stand-specific H and H+H move names should be checked on the relevant official Stand or Evolution card where available.
awakening.relatedRaidsSub: Official raid locations and shop notes
awakening.relatedTierListSub: Pick the best Stand before investing in awakening

nightVampire.metaTitle: Night Vampire / Vampire Facts | Bizarre Lineage Official Source Check
nightVampire.metaDescription: A source-checked Bizarre Lineage Vampire page: Stone Mask, Elder Vampire, official Vampire moves, and what is not publicly confirmed about Night Vampire.
nightVampire.intro: The public official Trello confirms base Vampire information. It does not currently publish a complete Night Vampire upgrade route in the checked public cards.
nightVampire.howStep1Body: The official Stone Mask card says Stone Mask can be bought from a shop or obtained from Common, Rare, and Legendary chests, and turns you into Vampire upon use.
nightVampire.howStep2Body: The official Vampire card says to talk to Elder Vampire in Dio's Chapel and complete his quests to unlock abilities.
nightVampire.howStep3Body: The checked public official cards do not publish a separate Night Vampire upgrade card, Level 50 upgrade route, cooldown table, or best Stand pairing.
nightVampire.strength1: + Stone Mask turns the player into Vampire on use.
nightVampire.strength2: + Vampire has passive regeneration and a red meter for bonus regeneration.
nightVampire.strength3: + Space Ripper Stingy Eyes is listed on the official Vampire card.
nightVampire.strength4: + Leeching Terror is listed on the official Vampire card.
nightVampire.strength5: + Flash Freeze is listed on the official Vampire card.
nightVampire.weakness1: - No public official Night Vampire upgrade card was found in the checked data.
nightVampire.weakness2: - No official Night Vampire keybind or cooldown table is published in the checked public card.
nightVampire.weakness3: - No official best Stand pairing is published for Vampire or Night Vampire.
nightVampire.weakness4: - No official separate Night Vampire sun-damage rule is published in the checked public card.
nightVampire.weakness5: - Any route beyond base Vampire needs official or dated in-game verification.
nightVampire.ability1Desc: The official Vampire card describes pressurized fluid jets from the eyes that stun and burn the target.
nightVampire.ability2Desc: The official Vampire card describes a grab that takes health from the opponent and heals the user.
nightVampire.ability3Desc: The official Vampire card describes an uppercut into a freeze and slam that stuns the opponent on the ground.
nightVampire.ability4Desc: The checked public official cards do not publish Night Vampire-only moves, cooldowns, or keybinds.
nightVampire.pairing1Reason: Pairing suggestions are site-maintained planner notes, not official Vampire data.
nightVampire.pairing2Reason: Pairing suggestions are site-maintained planner notes, not official Vampire data.
nightVampire.pairing3Reason: Pairing suggestions are site-maintained planner notes, not official Vampire data.
nightVampire.pairing4Reason: Pairing suggestions are site-maintained planner notes, not official Vampire data.
nightVampire.faqQ1: Is Night Vampire officially documented on the public Trello?
nightVampire.faqA1: The checked public cards confirm base Vampire, Stone Mask, Elder Vampire, and Vampire moves. They do not publish a separate complete Night Vampire upgrade route.
nightVampire.faqQ2: Can I treat old Night Vampire guides as official?
nightVampire.faqA2: No. Treat them as community guidance unless they cite a current official card or dated in-game testing.
nightVampire.faqQ3: What should this page show until official data exists?
nightVampire.faqA3: It should show official base Vampire facts and clearly mark Night Vampire route details as unconfirmed.
nightVampire.relatedVampireSubBody: Base Vampire stats and move data with planner boundaries
nightVampire.relatedHamonSubBody: Official Hamon card plus site-maintained scores

Guides.standChancesIntro: How this site groups Stands by local rarity labels for browsing, plus what the public official Trello does and does not confirm about Stand acquisition. Note: tier and rarity labels on this page are site-maintained, not official balance data.
Guides.statsIntro: What every stat does in Bizarre Lineage, plus site-maintained presets for PvP, PvE, and beginner builds. Stat names cross-reference the official Trello; allocation suggestions are local planner notes.
Guides.bestBuildsIntro: Site-maintained build ideas for Bizarre Lineage. These are planner suggestions, not official developer recommendations or confirmed stat allocations. Click any build to load it directly in the Build Planner.

WorldEvents.metaTitle: Bizarre Lineage World Events (May 2026) — Official Trello Listing
WorldEvents.metaDescription: The latest official Bizarre Lineage World Events listing. The public Trello currently names Graveyard Uprising and Deathmatch, with world events spawning every 20 minutes.
WorldEvents.heroIntro: The public official Trello currently describes World Events as limited-time, server-wide activities that offer unique gameplay and rewards. That same card states that the World Event system spawns every 20 minutes.
WorldEvents.eventsTitle: Events Currently Listed on the Official Trello
WorldEvents.graveyardDesc: A survival mode where players fight waves of NPCs without dying, all within a strict time limit.
WorldEvents.deathmatchDesc: A team-based PvP mode where two squads face off, each player starting with only 2 lives. The goal is to eliminate the opposing team.
WorldEvents.confirm1: World Events are limited-time, server-wide activities that offer unique gameplay and rewards.
WorldEvents.confirm2: The public Trello currently states that the World Event system spawns every 20 minutes.
WorldEvents.confirm3: Graveyard Uprising is described as a survival event where players fight waves of NPCs without dying inside a strict time limit.
WorldEvents.confirm4: Deathmatch is described as a team-based PvP mode where two squads fight with only 2 lives per player.
WorldEvents.notSpecifiedTitle: What the Official Card Does Not Specify
WorldEvents.notSpec1: The card does not publish a reward table for either event.
WorldEvents.notSpec2: The card does not list map rotation, queue rules, or matchmaking thresholds beyond the short event descriptions above.
WorldEvents.notSpec3: The current public card only names these two event types, so older third-party guides may no longer reflect the latest official listing.
WorldEvents.sourceTrelloSub: Primary source for the current World Events listing and cadence.
WorldEvents.sourceRobloxSub: Use this alongside the Trello when checking whether a live server matches the latest public listing.
WorldEvents.relatedItemsSub: Cross-check Lucky Arrow and other item acquisition routes
WorldEvents.relatedTrelloSub: Open the board directly and compare raw official cards

TierList.metaTitle: Bizarre Lineage Planner Tier List | Site-Maintained Rankings
TierList.metaDescription: A site-maintained Bizarre Lineage Stand tier list for planning. Move names and Stand references are checked against official Trello; rankings are not official balance values.
TierList.heroIntro: Site-maintained ranking data for all {count} indexed Stands in Bizarre Lineage. Filter by Overall, PvP, or PvE performance. This is planner guidance, not an official tier list.
TierList.howItWorksTitle: How the Site Planner Tier List Works
TierList.howItWorks1: This tier list ranks all {count} Stands in Bizarre Lineage across three categories: Overall, PvP, and PvE. Rankings are based on community testing and planner data — they are not official game balance values.
TierList.howItWorks2: Each Stand is evaluated on 6 local planner dimensions: Damage, Combo, CC, AoE, Mobility, and Sustain. PvP/PvE weighting is a site formula, not official developer balance data.
TierList.howItWorks3: S+ Tier means strongest under this site formula. S Tier means strong planner picks. Lower tiers can still work in-game depending on skill, matchup, and updates.

faq.a1: No. This tier list is maintained by the site using planner data and gameplay testing notes. Official balance data is not published by the developer. Move names and Stand information are checked against the official Trello board where available.
faq.q2: How often is the tier list updated?
faq.a2: We review rankings after significant updates. Official Trello changes are checked first; scoring changes are site-maintained and should be treated as planner guidance.
faq.q3: Why is my Stand ranked lower than I expected?
faq.a3: Tier rankings reflect this site's formula, not a universal truth. A skilled player can make many Stands work; the ranking is an average planning shortcut.
faq.q4: What's the difference between PvP and PvE tiers?
faq.a4: PvP and PvE tiers use different local planner weights. They are not official mode-specific developer ratings.

Codes.metaDescription: Bizarre Lineage codes for May 2026, with official Trello-listed codes separated from community-reported watchlist entries.
Codes.lastVerified: Last checked: {date} · official Trello codes card and Roblox page
Codes.verificationBody: The public official Trello codes card currently lists four codes. Community-reported Update 1 and milestone codes are kept in a watchlist below and are not presented as official active codes until confirmed through an official source or dated in-game testing.
Codes.step2: Open the in-game chat (press / or click the chat icon)
Codes.step3: Type !code 30kLikes (replace with any official code above)
Codes.step4: Press Enter — rewards are added to your inventory instantly
Codes.why1Body: Codes are case-sensitive. 30kLikes works, 30klikes does not.
Codes.why2Body: Each code can only be used once per account.
Codes.why3Body: Codes expire without warning. Check this page for the latest status.
Codes.why4Body: Only treat codes as official when they appear in official channels such as the Trello, Discord, or Roblox page. Community tracker entries can be useful leads, but they need separate verification.
Codes.expiredEmpty: No expired official Trello-listed codes are currently shown here. Community-reported entries stay in the watchlist until verified.
Codes.sourcesIntro: Official and community-tracker links are separated so confirmed codes and watchlist leads do not get mixed together:

faq.q1: How do I redeem codes in Bizarre Lineage?
faq.a1: Open the in-game chat and type !code followed by the code. For example: !code 30kLikes. Codes are case-sensitive.
faq.q2: Why is my code not working?
faq.a2: Codes are case-sensitive, may be expired, may already be redeemed, or may only be community-reported rather than officially confirmed.
faq.q3: Where do new codes come from?
faq.a3: New official codes should come from official channels such as the Trello, Discord, and Roblox game page. This page keeps community-reported codes in a separate watchlist until they can be verified.

Codes.exploreTierListSub: Site-maintained planner ranking, not official balance
Codes.exploreBuildPlannerSub: Plan your Stand + Style + Sub combo
Codes.exploreStandsSub: Browse all 17 Stands with moves and stats
Codes.watchlistBody: These entries are reported by public code trackers, but they are not on the checked public official Trello codes card. Treat them as unverified until they are confirmed by an official source or dated in-game testing.

Stands.metaTitle: All Stands in Bizarre Lineage | Database & Stats
Stands.metaDescription: {name} reference for Bizarre Lineage: official move/obtainment notes where available, plus clearly labeled site-maintained planner scores and build suggestions.
Stands.heroIntro: Browse all available Stands in Bizarre Lineage. Each page combines official move and obtainment notes with this site's planner scores and community build suggestions.
Stands.detailMetaTitle: {name} Reference — Moves, Rarity & Planner Builds | Bizarre Lineage
Stands.detailMetaDescription: {name} reference for Bizarre Lineage: official move and obtainment notes where available, plus clearly labeled site-maintained planner scores and build suggestions.
Stands.detailHowToGetTitle: How to Get {name} in Bizarre Lineage
Stands.detailNoCounters: No strong counters in current dataset.
Stands.detailNoCounteredBy: No hard counters in current dataset.
Stands.detailCtaBody: Test your Stand + Style + Sub combo before committing. Most players skip this and regret it.
Stands.detailProsConsIntro: These strengths and weaknesses are site-maintained planner notes, not official Trello labels.
Stands.faqQ3: What is the best build for {name}?
Stands.faqA1: {name} is currently rated {tierOverall} Tier overall in our planner dataset, with {tierPvp} in PvP and {tierPve} in PvE. {meta}
Stands.faqA2Special: {name} is obtained via {obtainMethod}. This is an evolution-only Stand — follow the evolution quest path instead of using a Stand Arrow.
Stands.faqA2Normal: {name} is obtained via {obtainMethod}. The public official Stand Arrow card says Stand Arrows can be found on the ground or obtained from Common, Rare, and Legendary chests.
Stands.faqA3: Our planner recommends pairing {name} with {style} fighting style and {sub} sub-ability for PvP. Use the Build Planner to test different combinations and find what works for your playstyle.
Stands.faqA4WithCountersAndStruggle: In the site planner dataset, {name} is strong against {counters}. However, it struggles against {counteredBy}. This is not an official matchup table.
Stands.faqA4WithCountersOnly: In the site planner dataset, {name} is strong against {counters}. This is not an official matchup table.
Stands.faqA4None: {name} does not have strong counters in the current site dataset. Treat this as planner guidance, not an official matchup claim.
Stands.faqA5First: Yes. {name} is part of the evolution chain: {chain}. You evolve into {name} from the previous stage.
Stands.faqA5Last: This site lists {name} in the evolution chain: {chain}. Check the relevant official Trello evolution card before relying on any exact quest step.
Stands.overviewParagraph: The public official Trello is used here for move names and obtainment notes. On this site, {name} is grouped under the local {rarity} label, obtained primarily via {obtainMethod}, and currently placed at {tier} Tier in the planner dataset.
Stands.howToGetSpecial: {name} is obtained via {obtainMethod}. This is listed on the site as an evolution-only Stand. Follow the relevant official quest or evolution path instead of relying on a normal Arrow roll.
Stands.howToGetNormal: {name} is obtained via {obtainMethod}. The public Trello confirms the obtainment method, but it does not publish exact drop percentages on this page.
Stands.statBreakdownP1Intro: {name} scores {damage}/10 in Damage and {combo}/10 in Combo potential.
Stands.statDamageHigh: This is high under the local planner formula, not an official damage number.
Stands.statDamageMid: This is mid-range under the local planner formula, not an official damage number.
Stands.statDamageLow: This is lower under the local planner formula, not an official damage number.
Stands.statBreakdownP2Intro: For crowd control, {name} sits at {cc}/10 CC with {aoe}/10 AoE coverage.
Stands.statCcHigh: Its local CC score is high in the planner dataset.
Stands.statCcMid: Its local CC score is moderate in the planner dataset.
Stands.statCcLow: Its local CC score is low in the planner dataset.
Stands.statMobilityHigh: {name} has a high local planner mobility score.
Stands.statMobilityLow: {name} has a low local planner mobility score.
Stands.statMobilityMid: Its local mobility score is moderate.
Stands.statSustainHigh: Its sustain score is also high in the site dataset.
Stands.statSustainOther: Its sustain score should be treated as a planning estimate.
Stands.statBreakdownP4Intro: {name} is grouped under {rarity} on this site. The {awakening} awakening value shown in planner data is site-maintained; the public official Inner World card confirms Level 50 and Stand Conjuration 100 as the awakening option requirement.
Stands.statAwakeningHigh: Use that planner value only as an internal comparison point, not as an official per-Stand requirement.
Stands.statAwakeningLow: Use that planner value only as an internal comparison point, not as an official per-Stand requirement.
Stands.mistake1MobLow: Trying to chase opponents without a gap closer. {name} has {mobility}/10 mobility — you lose chases against faster Stands. Use your CC to force fights on your terms instead.
Stands.mistake1DmgHigh: Over-reading local damage scores. {name} scores high in this planner, but that is not an official one-combo guarantee.
Stands.mistake1Default: Picking {name} without testing the suggested {style} pairing first. The planner suggests it to cover {name}'s local {gap} gap, not because developers recommend it.
Stands.mistake2HasCountersHighMob: Treating a planner matchup as official. {name} is marked as struggling into {firstCounter} in the site dataset; verify in-game before making decisions.
Stands.mistake2HasCountersLowMob: Treating a planner matchup as official. {name} is marked as struggling into {firstCounter} in the site dataset; verify in-game before making decisions.
Stands.mistake2NoCounters: Treating planner values as official. The {awakening} awakening value for {name} is site-maintained; verify public requirements on the Inner World source before relying on it.
Stands.mistake3DiffTiers: Using one planner context everywhere. {name} is rated {pvpTier} in PvP and {pveTier} in PvE by this site, but these are local categories, not official tiers.
Stands.mistake3SameTiers: Skipping planner comparison. Even when local tiers match, testing style and sub combinations can surface tradeoffs.
Stands.bestPvpDescription: Our local planner currently pairs {name} with {style} and {sub}. This is a site recommendation, not an official balance callout.

Items.metaTitle: Bizarre Lineage Items | Official Trello Source Links
Items.metaDescription: Bizarre Lineage item reference with official Trello source links where available. Effects, obtainment notes, rarity labels, and exact rates are not treated as official unless a linked card supports them.
Items.heroIntro: Source-linked reference for {count} Bizarre Lineage items. Each card links to the public Trello source where available, and uncertain details should be verified in-game.

related.raidsSub: Official raid shops and location notes

Items.sourceBoundary: Item names, effects, obtainment notes, and rarity labels are linked back to public Trello cards where available. Exact rates are only treated as confirmed when the official card prints them; otherwise this page avoids inventing numbers.

Raids.metaTitle: Bizarre Lineage Raids — All Raid Shops & Locations (Official Trello)
Raids.metaDescription: All 4 Bizarre Lineage raid bosses (Avdol, Kira, Jotaro, DIO) with the verified Trello locations and complete raid-shop listings — token costs and stock for every item.
Raids.heroIntro: Raids are an 8-player cooperative game mode. Completing a raid earns rewards and Raid Shop Tokens, which are spent in that raid's exclusive shop. There are 4 raids on the official Trello.
Raids.verifiedBody: Boss HP, level, mechanics, and grading are not documented on the public Trello. This page lists only what the Trello confirms: locations, NPC names, and the full shop catalogue (with stock and approximate token cost).

faq.a1: Four: Avdol, Kira (Bites the Dust), Jotaro (Chumbo), and DIO. Each has its own shop and token type.
faq.a2: Up to 8 players per raid (per the official Trello).
faq.q3: What can I buy with raid tokens?
faq.a3: Each raid shop has a different mix of accessories, traits, Stand Arrows, Lucky Arrows, and Legendary Chests. The DIO Raid shop also sells the Shadow Axe and Stop Sign weapons.
faq.q4: Are boss HP, level, and mechanics on the Trello?
faq.a4: No — those details are not documented on the public official Trello. We do not list speculative numbers here.

locations.kira: Bus Stop 1, around the Rengatei shop (Morioh Station area)
locations.dio: Bus Stop 18, near the Elder Vampire in the castle (Graveyard area)

Raids.detailMetaDescription: Verified Trello details for the {boss} raid: location, NPC, and the full shop catalogue with stock and approximate token cost for every item.
Raids.detailOverviewBody1: The {boss} raid is one of four cooperative raids on the official Trello. Talk to {npc} at {location} to start it.
Raids.detailOverviewBody2: Up to 8 players can join per the Trello's raids overview. Completing the raid awards {boss} Tokens, which are spent in the shop below.
Raids.detailShopFooter: Token costs come from the official Trello and are listed as approximate (~). Confirm in-game.
Raids.detailFaqQ1: Where do I find the {boss} raid?
Raids.detailFaqQ2: Does the Trello list boss HP, level, or mechanics?
Raids.detailFaqA2: No. The public official Trello does not document boss stats, mechanics, grading, or recommended Stands. We do not include speculative numbers here.
Raids.detailCtaBody: Try Stand + Style + Sub combos in the planner.

Footer.disclaimer: Official move and progression notes are cross-checked against the public Trello board. Planner rankings and build notes are site-maintained.

Home.heroSubtitle: Cross-check official Trello move data, compare site-maintained planner rankings, and verify official links before you spend your resources.
Home.searchPlaceholder: Search for a Stand (e.g. Whitesnake, Made in Heaven)...
Home.updateBannerBody: Roblox shows Update 1 live as of May 9, 2026. This site separates official Trello data from community-reported Update 1 codes and meta changes.
Home.latestCodesSub: Official Trello-listed codes only; community reports stay in Codes watchlist.
Home.latestGameUpdatesSub: Official Roblox and Trello signals first; community reports stay clearly labeled.
Home.metaWatchSub: Unconfirmed Update 1 names we are watching. They do not change planner rankings until verified through official sources or in-game testing.
Home.step1Body: Choose from 17 Stands in this site's local dataset. Each entry keeps official move names but uses site-maintained planner scores.
Home.step2Body: Pair your Stand with a fighting style (Boxing, Kendo, Karate) and a sub-ability (Hamon, Vampire, Cyborg) to test planner combinations.
Home.step3Body: See planner estimates across 5 dimensions, save them to your Vault, and compare local build profiles side by side.
Home.watchSub: Beginner guide with official facts and clearly marked unknowns.
Home.patchNote: Move names, obtain methods, and progression notes are cross-checked against the public official Trello board. Rankings and planner scores are site-maintained notes, not official balance values.

faq.q1: What is the best Stand in Bizarre Lineage for PvP?
faq.a1: There is no official developer tier list. This site currently ranks Stands through a local planner formula; use it as planning guidance and verify in-game after updates.
faq.q2: Are there any active Bizarre Lineage codes right now?
faq.a2: This site now tracks 15 codes: 4 are listed on the official Trello and 11 are community-reported Update 1 or milestone codes. Type !code followed by the code in the in-game chat to redeem, and check the Codes page for the source label on each entry.
faq.q3: How do I get a specific Stand like Whitesnake or Made in Heaven?
faq.a3: The public Trello confirms that you unlock your first Stand with a Stand Arrow. Lucky Arrow does not raise Stand rarity; it guarantees a random skin on your current Stand. Evolution stands such as Made in Heaven follow their own quest or evolution paths on the official Trello.
faq.q4: What is the best fighting style and sub-ability combo?
faq.a4: The best combo depends on your Stand and playstyle. Any recommendations on this site are local planner estimates, not official developer recommendations.
faq.q5: How does the Build Planner scoring system work?
faq.a5: Our build planner estimates a Stand + Fighting Style + Sub-Ability combo across 5 dimensions: PvP, PvE, Survival, Mobility, and Cost Efficiency. The numbers come from the site's local dataset and weighting rules, not from official game balance values.
